building_row.open_questions: Verify whether the target residence faces the pool or courtyard side rather than the livelier Sunset frontage. Confirm whether the East Tower annual-lease apartments feel operationally separate enough from the serviced-residence side of the property for your taste. Ask specifically about sound insulation, elevator traffic, and any persistent in-unit appliance issues, since one long-term resident noted an ongoing washer problem.
building_row.notes: AKA is the strongest operations-forward building so far. Public official materials and listing platforms align on 24-hour resident services, package handling, valet and included parking, pool deck, private cinema, high-tech fitness center, controlled access, and in-unit laundry. Pricing transparency is also unusually good: the official FAQ discloses trash, metered utilities, pet fee and pet rent, deposit range, parking terms, overnight guest parking, renter’s insurance, and package-delivery workflow. The main caution is quiet. This is directly on the iconic Sunset Strip and shares an address with retail and serviced-residence activity, so location noise risk remains high even though the official site also describes the apartments as offering a tranquil setting and RentCafe shows a strong 4.5 noise score. Management signal is better than average, not perfect. RentCafe has 48 reviews with strong staff and maintenance scores, plus a seven-year resident who still likes the environment, but a few critiques mention staff orientation and a long-running washer issue. On balance, this earns tour_candidate status because the service model, security, and fee transparency are materially stronger than most peers, but the exact unit placement still matters a lot for quiet.

evidence_rows.claim: The official FAQ publishes unusually detailed fee and policy terms including security deposit range, trash fee, metered utilities, no rent tax, pet fee and pet rent, included tandem parking, visitor parking charges, and renter’s insurance requirements.
evidence_rows.source_name: AKA West Hollywood Apartment Residences FAQ
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: Official FAQ says deposits range from $2,500 to 2 months rent, trash is $12 per month, water and power are metered, there are no taxes on monthly rent, and one tandem parking space plus valet are included.

evidence_rows.claim: The official FAQ confirms a 24-hour resident services team, 24-hour valet, resort-style pool and cabanas, private resident cinema, indoor-outdoor lounge, Wi-Fi-friendly common areas, and specific package-delivery procedures.
evidence_rows.source_name: AKA West Hollywood Apartment Residences FAQ
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: Smaller packages are logged at the West Tower front desk and delivered outside residents’ doors in two daily delivery windows, with immediate delivery for perishables.

evidence_rows.claim: The official home page places the building on the iconic Sunset Strip with a vibrant backdrop, while also describing each apartment as offering a tranquil setting.
evidence_rows.source_name: The Apartment Residences at AKA Home
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: Official copy supports both sides of the quiet question: premium location on Sunset, but an attempt at calm inside the residences.

evidence_rows.claim: Current official site materials identify Korman Communities as the operating company for the annual-lease apartment residences.
evidence_rows.source_name: The Apartment Residences at AKA Home
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: The official footer states ©2021 Korman Communities.

evidence_rows.claim: Apartments.com corroborates doorman and concierge service, video patrol, key-fob entry, EV charging, dog park, washer/dryer, dishwasher, office-ready layouts, and free resident parking.
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: The listing also shows 5.0 out of 5 with 2 renter reviews, 24-hour access, and a 9am-5pm office schedule.

evidence_rows.claim: RentCafe shows deep resident-review volume with generally strong scores for staff, maintenance, parking, and noise, but not a spotless record.
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: RentCafe shows 48 reviews, staff 5.0, maintenance 4.5, parking 5.0, and noise 4.5. Visible comments include praise from a seven-year resident, one complaint that staff is not client-oriented, and a note about a persistent washing-machine issue.

evidence_rows.claim: The annual-lease East Tower is part of a larger AKA mixed-use environment that includes retail and serviced residences, which improves service depth but may feel more hotel-like than a standard apartment building.
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: The FAQ distinguishes unfurnished annual East Tower apartments from monthly furnished West Tower serviced residences, and the home page notes the shared 8500 Sunset retail plaza.

packet_markdown:
# The Apartment Residences at AKA building packet

## Snapshot
- Building ID: aka-8500-sunset-8500-sunset-blvd
- Address: 8500 Sunset Blvd, WeHo 90069
- Primary URL: https://www.8500sunsetapartments.com/
- Management company: Korman Communities
- Recommended status: tour_candidate
- Last updated: 2026-03-25

## Scorecard
- Quiet: 3 (medium, evidence count 4)
- Management: 4 (medium, evidence count 4)
- Amenity: 5 (high, evidence count 7)
- Community stability: 3 (medium, evidence count 3)
- Location fit: 5
- Pricing transparency: 5

## Risks
- Structural noise risk: medium
- Location noise risk: high
- Security risk: low
- Pricing risk: low

## Evidence summary
- [official] The FAQ publishes detailed deposit, utility, parking, pet, insurance, and package terms. Source: AKA FAQ (https://www.8500sunsetapartments.com/faq.aspx).
- [official] The FAQ confirms 24-hour resident services, valet, pool and cabanas, cinema, and specific package-delivery windows. Source: AKA FAQ (https://www.8500sunsetapartments.com/faq.aspx).
- [official] The home page describes both a vibrant Sunset Strip setting and tranquil apartment interiors. Source: Home (https://www.8500sunsetapartments.com/).
- [review] Apartments.com corroborates doorman and concierge service, EV charging, dog park, and 24-hour access. Source: Apartments.com (https://www.apartments.com/aka-west-hollywood-apartment-residences-west-hollywood-ca/x4brlmm/).
- [review] RentCafe shows 48 reviews with strong staff, maintenance, parking, and noise scores, alongside a few service or appliance complaints. Source: RentCafe (https://www.rentcafe.com/apartments/ca/west-hollywood/the-apartment-residences-at-aka/default.aspx).

## Open questions
- Is the available unit materially shielded from Sunset Strip noise?
- Does the East Tower feel sufficiently separate from the serviced-residence side?
- Are there any unresolved appliance or elevator issues in the current available stack?

## Notes
AKA stands out for service depth, package handling, security, and fee clarity. The main thing that keeps it from being a perfect match is not operations. It is the Sunset Strip exposure and the mixed-use, somewhat hotel-like setting.
